# coreloop

A minimal async agent loop over any OpenAI-compatible API. Streams responses, executes tools, and loops until the LLM stops requesting tool calls.

## Install

```
pip install coreloop
# or with web tools:
pip install "coreloop[web]"
```

## Quickstart

```python
import asyncio
from coreloop import Agent, Message

agent = Agent(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    base_url="https://api.openai.com/v1",
    api_key="sk-...",
    system="You are a helpful assistant.",
)

async def main():
    messages = [Message(role="user", content="Hello!")]
    async for msg in agent.run(messages):
        if not msg.partial:
            print(msg.content)

asyncio.run(main())
```

Default `base_url` is `http://localhost:11434/v1` (Ollama).

## Agent constructor

```python
Agent(
    model="qwen3:8b",
    base_url="http://localhost:11434/v1",
    api_key=None,
    system=None,
    tools=None,           # list of tool name strings or ToolInfo objects
    root=None,            # filesystem root for file tools
    http_request_timeout=300.0,
    tool_timeout=360.0,
    llm_timeout=300.0,
    hooks=None,           # AgentHooks instance
    llm_extra_body=None,  # extra fields passed to the LLM API body
    cache_dir="~/.cache/coreloop-llm-cache",  # None to disable
)
```

## AgentConfig (portable config bundle)

```python
from dataclasses import replace
from coreloop import AgentConfig, Agent

cfg = AgentConfig(model="qwen3:8b", system="Be terse.")
fast = replace(cfg, model="qwen3:0.6b", llm_timeout=30.0)
agent = Agent.from_config(fast)
```

Fields mirror `Agent.__init__` (except `hooks` and `backend`, which are stateful and passed to `from_config` separately).

## Profiles (coreloop.toml)

```toml
# ~/.coreloop.toml
[profiles.default]
model = "qwen3:8b"
base_url = "http://localhost:11434/v1"

[profiles.openai]
model = "gpt-4o-mini"
base_url = "https://api.openai.com/v1"
api_key = "{{OPENAI_API_KEY}}"   # resolved from env

[config.tool.bash]
max_chars = 20000
```

`{{VAR_NAME}}` in any string value is resolved from the environment.

```python
from coreloop.profiles import resolve_profile
cfg = resolve_profile("openai")          # returns AgentConfig
agent = Agent.from_profile("openai")     # shortcut
```

## Message

```python
from coreloop import Message

Message(role="user", content="Hello")
Message(role="assistant", content="Hi", tool_calls=[...])
Message(role="tool", content="result", tool_call_id="...", name="read")
```

Key fields: `role`, `content`, `tool_calls`, `tool_call_id`, `name`, `reasoning` (thinking models, not sent back to API), `partial` (True for streaming deltas), `usage`, `duration`, `model`.

## run() and message history

`agent.run(messages)` is an async generator. Skip `msg.partial is True` to see only complete messages.

```python
async for msg in agent.run(messages):
    if not msg.partial:
        print(msg.role, msg.content)

# Full conversation (system + all turns) after run completes:
history = agent.messages
```

Pass `agent.messages` to a new agent to continue the conversation.

## Stopping

```python
agent.stop()   # finish current turn then exit (on_after_agent is called)
agent.abort()  # cancel immediately (on_after_agent is NOT called)
agent.stopped  # True if either was called
agent.reset()  # clear history and stop flag between runs
```

## Built-in tools

Pass tool names as strings; file tools are scoped to `root`.

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `read` | Read a file |
| `ls` | List a directory |
| `edit` | Apply string replacements to a file |
| `grep` | Search files with ripgrep |
| `bash` | Run shell commands (dangerous patterns blocked) |
| `web_search` | Search via SearXNG JSON API |
| `web_fetch` | Fetch a URL, returns markdown |

```python
agent = Agent(model="qwen3:8b", tools=["read", "grep", "bash"], root="/my/project")
```

`web_search` and `web_fetch` require `pip install "coreloop[web]"`.

## Custom tools via @tool

```python
from coreloop import tool, Agent

@tool
async def get_weather(city: str) -> str:
    """Return current weather for a city."""
    return f"Sunny in {city}"

# Pass by name (resolved from global registry) or by object:
agent = Agent(model="qwen3:8b", tools=["get_weather"])
# or
agent = Agent(model="qwen3:8b", tools=[get_weather])
```

`@tool` infers the JSON Schema from type annotations. Supported types: `str`, `int`, `float`, `bool`, `list[T]`, `dict`, `Optional[T]`, `Literal[...]`.

Custom name/description:
```python
@tool(name="weather", description="Get current weather for a city.")
async def get_weather(city: str) -> str: ...
```

## AgentHooks

Subclass `AgentHooks` to intercept any lifecycle event. Hook exceptions are logged and swallowed — they cannot crash the agent.

```python
from coreloop import AgentHooks, Agent

class MyHooks(AgentHooks):
    async def on_before_agent(self, agent): ...
    async def on_after_agent(self, agent): ...
    async def on_before_turn(self, agent): ...
    async def on_after_turn(self, agent): ...

    async def on_before_llm(self, agent):
        return None  # or return a Message to skip the LLM call

    async def on_after_llm(self, agent, message):
        return None  # or return a Message to replace it in history

    async def on_before_tool(self, agent, name, args):
        return None  # or return a str to skip tool execution

    async def on_after_tool(self, agent, name, args, result):
        return None  # or return a str to replace result in history

agent = Agent(model="qwen3:8b", hooks=MyHooks())
```

Built-in hook: `MaxTurnsHook(n)` — calls `agent.stop()` after n turns.

```python
from coreloop import MaxTurnsHook
agent = Agent(model="qwen3:8b", hooks=MaxTurnsHook(5))
```

## make_tools / make_web_tools

Lower-level constructors returning `list[ToolInfo]`:

```python
from coreloop import make_tools, make_web_tools, make_bash_tool

file_tools = make_tools(root="/my/project")  # read, ls, edit, grep
web_tools = make_web_tools()                 # web_search, web_fetch
bash = make_bash_tool("/my/project")

agent = Agent(model="qwen3:8b", tools=[*file_tools, bash])
```

## CLI

```bash
uv run core --model qwen3:8b                          # interactive REPL
uv run core -p "summarise this" --model qwen3:8b      # one-shot
uv run core --tools read,grep --root . --model qwen3:8b
uv run core --think --model qwen3:8b                  # thinking mode
uv run core --profile openai -p "hello"               # named profile
```
